title: ceratocephalus grandiflora
intro: Ceratocephalus grandiflora, also known as large-flowered Ceratocephalus, is a plant species in the family Apiaceae, commonly found in mountainous regions of Europe. The plant has been used for its medicinal properties, particularly its seeds, which contain a volatile oil that has been used to treat digestive issues and respiratory problems. The seeds of Ceratocephalus grandiflora have been reported to have health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties, which may help alleviate symptoms of colds, flu, and other respiratory infections. Traditionally, the seeds are used in herbal preparations, such as infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, to take advantage of their medicinal properties.
